opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: There's this hard lump on the bottom of my foot that suddenly feels like I'm stepping on a sharp piece of glass every time I walk.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: A hard lump on my sole that has become sharply painful to stand/walk on.

symptom_narrative.onset: a firm lump on the sole for months (thought it was a callus); acutely painful today after standing
symptom_narrative.character: hard rough lump on the sole; sharp 'stepping on glass' pain on pressure
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: severe on pressure; none at rest; limping
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: rough surface; tiny black dots when the surface is pared; skin lines interrupted over it; no fever
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: hobbling on the side of the foot

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: long periods standing; uses public showers

objective_data_offered.value: a ~1cm hard rough lump on the ball of the foot; the skin lines are interrupted across it and there are pinpoint black dots; sharp pain on direct pressure, mild on side-squeeze; no surrounding redness/pus
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: camera view; cannot pare or probe
